description:
An open-source alternative to Claude Cowork, powered by OpenCode

OpenWork is an extensible, open-source “Claude Work” style system for knowledge workers.
It’s a native desktop app that runs OpenCode under the hood, but presents it as a clean, guided workflow:
- pick a workspace
- start a run
- watch progress + plan updates
- approve permissions when needed
- reuse what works (templates + skills)
The goal: make “agentic work” feel like a product, not a terminal.
Highlights
- Refined the app shell and sidebar UX: removed the artifacts rail, pinned footer actions, preserved workspace actions visibility, and improved session title/timestamp readability.
- Added settings support for authorized-folder management and server-backed empty states to make worker setup and first-run guidance clearer.
Fixes & polish
- Restored composer action labels and tightened sidebar layout behavior to prevent overflow and truncation issues.
- Moved Den signup Loops sync into the controller to improve reliability of signup tracking.
Packaging / ops
- Updated release automation for Daytona snapshot publishing authentication.
- Bumped all packages to 0.11.175 and refreshed AUR metadata/lockfile consistency.
Compare:
https://github.com/different-ai/openwork/compare/v0.11.174...v0.11.175
